rulings.Case Title: PETER GALASSI et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 9, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Central to Crane Co.'s motion is the distinction between original equipment and replacement parts. However, the special interrogatories that Crane Co. propounded to address that issue use the phrase "original or replacement" parts. That form of discovery does not require plaintiff to delineate whether the evidence relates to an original or replacement product. As a consequence, this form of discovery does not demonstrate that plaintiff cannot prove exposure to original equipment or replacement equipment. Plaintiff's discovery responses point to Crane Co. discovery that admits that its products contained asbestos that needed to be replaced and emitted respirable asbestos. (Disputed Fact 26.) Plaintiff's also point to decedent's deposition testimony that he worked with and around Crane valves. Crane has failed to shift the burden of proof in that Crane has not demonstrated that plaintiff is unable to establish that the Crane valves plaintiff worked with and around did not expose him to asbestos that was placed on the product by Crane. Crane Co.'s motion for summary adjudication of issues 1 through 17 is denied for failure to shift the burden of proof and to the extent applicable, the issue presented does not eliminate an entire cause of action. The Court grants Crane Co.'s motion for summary adjudication as to issues 18 (intentional tort), 19 (successor liability) and 20 (punitive damages). Defendant shifted the burden of proof and plaintiff has not presented evidence raising a triable issue of fact. The Court has on Crane Co.'s evidentiary objections 1 through 13. The Court did not rule on the objections to the Burger Declaration because Crane did not shift the burden of proof as to the summary judgment motion and issues 1 through 17. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: PETER GALASSI et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 9, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT CLEAVER-BROOKS', A DIVISION OF AQUA-CHEM, INC., MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of proof. Plaintiff's testimony established that he worked with defendant's product and defendant's effort to distinguish between original and replacement parts fails for two reasons. Defendant does not comply with California Rule of Court 3.1350 and defendant's questions fail to ask a specific question addressing decedent's exposure to original gaskets as opposed to replacement gaskets. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ONE CLARENCE PLACE OWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNI VS. MARTIN PROPERTIES, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 9, 2008 LINE 3. CROSS-DEFENDANT GOLD RIVER CONTRACTORS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Neither the complaint nor the general contractor's cross-complaint alleged misconduct by this party. To the contrary, the only specific allegation in paragraph 9 of the complaint, page 4 lines 3-5, attributes the problem to "defective design, construction and/or selection of materials for the framing of the structure" as the cause for the interior wall and fixture cracking. These allegations do not implicate the drywall work of this cross-defendant. Neither the Bzieff Declaration nor the Kennedy Declaration are admissible to establish that there is "a corner bead pops" problem. Neither witness has provided a factual foundation for the contention made or the qualifications to give such an opinion. Cross-complainant's request to allow plaintiff to amend the complaint is denied for lack of standing to make the request and as contrary to CCP section 437c and for the further reason that any amendment appears to be barred by the 10 year statute of limitations. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLENE AMMANN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 9, 2008 LINE 4. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. PLAINTIFF HAS NO CLAIM FOR GENERAL NEGLIGENCE. GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 815 AND THE UNDISPUTED FACTS DEMONSTRATE THIS IS NOT A DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY. FIELDER V CITY OF GLENDALE (1977) 71 CAL APP 3D 719, 724 FN.4. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LAUARALEE BROWN MARKUS VS. WOMEN'S INTITIATIVE FOR SELF EMPLOYMENT,A et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 9, 2008 LINE 9. DEFENDANTS WOMEN'S INTITIATIVE FOR SELF EMPLOYMENT, JULIE ABRAMS AND DAVID VENEZIANO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS. WORKERS' COMPENSATION IS THE EXCLUSIVE REMEDY AS IT RELATES TO THE INDIVIDUALS. THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE D.F.E.H. RIGHT TO SUE LETTER. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES AND HAS EXHAUSTED HER ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE REASONABLY RELATED TO PLAINTIFF'S D.F.E.H. COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION AS RELATES TO W.I.S.E. BECAUSE DISABLITY DISCRIMINATION CLAIMS ARE NOT A NORMAL PART OF THE EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP. =(302/PJM/X-MS)
